title: PACO Renderer Manifest
description: Schema for a single renderer's renderer.json manifest (paco/renderers/<name>/renderer.json). Validates ONE renderer in isolation — registry-level concerns that span multiple renderer.json files (uid uniqueness across the whole set, confirming a base renderer exists for each file_mode) are NOT expressible here and are deliberately left to the boot-time registry-loading code instead.

description.description: Short, human-readable description of what this renderer does.

uid.description: Globally unique identifier for this renderer. Uniqueness ACROSS the whole renderer set is a registry-loading concern, not enforceable by this single-document schema.

selection_type.description: Whether this renderer applies to a single-item selection or a multi-item selection. Exact match required against the selection's own derived value — see paco/renderers/matcher.js's gate step.

file_mode.description: Whether this renderer applies to text or binary content, per the existing on-prem text/binary classification (paco/file-handler-detect.js). Exact match required, same as selection_type.

binary_category.description: Generic binary sub-category this renderer handles (rung-2 / generic-category matching — see matcher.js). Only meaningful when file_mode is "binary"; forbidden when file_mode is "text" — see the file_mode/binary_category conditional below. May coexist with file_type, in which case it is purely informational and plays no role in matching (a renderer with file_type is always a rung-1 match, never additionally offered as a rung-2 match).

file_type.description: Specific file type(s) this renderer handles (rung-1 / file-specific matching). Either a single non-empty string (e.g. "png", "md") or a non-empty array of such strings for a closely-related family (e.g. ["jpeg","jpg","png","gif"]). A renderer declaring exactly one string here is treated as more specific than one declaring an array, for preselection purposes — see matcher.js's preselection priority order. Legal under both file_mode "text" and "binary" — there is no requirement to also declare binary_category alongside it (e.g. a markdown renderer needs only file_mode:"text" + file_type:"md").

oneOf.$comment: uniqueItems is left false here because JSON Schema's built-in uniqueItems check is exact-string (case-sensitive) and cannot itself enforce a case-INSENSITIVE no-duplicates rule (e.g. rejecting ["png","PNG"]). That weaker case-sensitive form alone wasn't requested. A case-insensitive duplicate check is a renderer-loading-time concern (or a custom $comment-only convention authors are trusted to follow), not something this schema enforces.

allOf.$comment: binary_category is forbidden under file_mode "text" — by design, never supported (e.g. SVG is text but conventionally image-like; this is a deliberate, accepted gap rather than something the schema bends to accommodate).
